tool_args.label: Wabtec Corporation Purchase Agreement Procurement
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North Railroad (MNR) seeks MTA Board approval on behalf of itself, Long Island Rail Road ("LIRR") and New York City Transit for a purchase agreement to include various parts, components, and repair services for MNR's M-3, Shoreliner, M-7 and M-8 railcars and locomotives; LIRR's M-3, M-7, and M-9 railcars, C-3 coach cars, and diesel-electric/dual mode locomotives; and all NYC Transit subway cars.

payload.claim_text: Metro-North Railroad (MNR) seeks MTA Board approval on behalf of itself, Long Island Rail Road ('LIRR') and New York City Transit for a purchase agreement to include various parts, components, and repair services for MNR's M-3, Shoreliner, M-7 and M-8 railcars and locomotives; LIRR's M-3, M-7, and M-9 railcars, C-3 coach cars, and diesel-electric/dual mode locomotives; and all NYC Transit subway cars.
payload.description: Joint procurement for railcar parts, components, and repair services from Wabtec Corporation

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North Railroad (MNR) seeks MTA Board approval on behalf of itself, Long Island Rail Road ('LIRR') and New York City Transit for a purchase agreement to include various parts, components, and repair services

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Nouveau Elevator Escalator Maintenance Contract
tool_args.raw_text: award a five year competitively solicited miscellaneous service contract conducted through the request for proposal process to Nouveau Elevator Industries, LLC in the estimated amount of $31,768,434. The contract includes ongoing maintenance, inspection, and repair services for 53 escalators located in Grand Central Terminal, Metro-North's White Plains station, Penn Station, and other LIRR stations in Nassau, Suffolk, and Queens Counties.

payload.claim_text: award a five year competitively solicited miscellaneous service contract conducted through the request for proposal process to Nouveau Elevator Industries, LLC in the estimated amount of $31,768,434. tool_args.label: LIRR Laser Train Technology
tool_args.raw_text: Two years ago, LIRR launched an industry first laser train that zaps slippery pectin off the rails, tackles low adhesion, minimizes flat wheels... In 2021, LIRR cleaned 116% more miles of track than it did in 2020. It partnered with the manufacturer Laser Precision Solutions to further improve the technology.

payload.claim_text: Two years ago, LIRR launched an industry first laser train that zaps slippery pectin off the rails, tackles low adhesion, minimizes flat wheels which reduces the number of cars out of service. In 2021, LIRR cleaned 116% more miles of track than it did in 2020. It partnered with the manufacturer Laser Precision Solutions to further improve the technology. First, it improved the laser train's cleaning efficiency by 25% while also increasing train speed. Second, it improved how and when it deployed these trains.
payload.description: LIRR's industry-first laser train for rail cleaning

evidence_refs.source_quote: Two years ago, LIRR launched an industry first laser train that zaps slippery pectin off the rails, tackles low adhesion, minimizes flat wheels which reduces the number of cars out of service
